channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden commutes sentence for Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier, convicted in killing of FBI agents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With just moments left before he leaves office, President Joe Biden commuted the life sentence of indigenous activist Leonard Peltier, who was convicted in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump plans to rename Gulf of Mexico to 'Gulf of America,' reports say
item.description: Karoline Leavitt announced one of the day-one executive actions President-elect Trump will take is renaming the Gulf of Mexico to "Gulf of America," NBC announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump will issue executive orders to remake immigration policies but they will face challenges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will sign a series of executive orders designed to beef up security at the southern border by relying more on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MLK Day kicks off at King's Atlanta congregation with daughter's warning about anti-woke rhetoric
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A massive 70-member choir belted out “Hallelujah” to open a Martin Luther King Jr. Day service Monday at his former congregation in Atlanta, followed by a stern message from his youngest daughter warning against anti-woke rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump returns to a changed Washington, this time with a full embrace from Republicans
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump takes the oath of office inside a tightly packed Capitol Rotunda, he will be surrounded by a very different Washington than he was eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH LIVE: Trump to be sworn in as 47th president
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington D.C., Donald Trump will take the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Trump inauguration to kick off 100-day rush
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to be sworn in at the Capitol on Monday, solidifying his stunning return to the White House and kicking off what is expected to be a busy 100-day rush focused on a handful of hot-button issues.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KREX 5

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: Do US adults support Trump's agenda? Here's what an AP-NORC poll shows
item.description: A poll finds that despite Donald Trump's claims of an “unprecedented and powerful mandate,” the incoming Republican president lacks broad support for some of his top priorities.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Thomson-Deveaux And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump inauguration: Mom of jogger allegedly killed by migrant praises friendship with incoming president
item.description: Patty Morin's quest for justice against migrant crime led to stiffer immigration laws and a trip to President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Millions across the US brace for plummeting temperatures and winter storms
item.description: Residents across the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine are bracing for dangerously low temperatures while the East Coast contends with a thick blanket of snow and the South prepares for a rare winter storm expected to disrupt travel from late Monday into Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Couple accused of stealing $315K from guest at Ritz-Carlton: Prosecutors
item.description: Police said the victim reported his hotel room had been ransacked and that $315,000 in cash had been stolen from his suitcase.
item.dc:creator: Kevin S. Held, NewsNation

item.title: Michigan 3-year-old accidentally shoots, injures self: Police
item.description: "Preliminary investigation indicates this was an accidentally self-inflicted injury," police wrote in a release.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rosendale, NewsNation

item.title: Can you spot black ice? What to do if you start sliding or skidding
item.description: Black ice forms without creating bubbles, meaning it blends in with the road and becomes hard to detect.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, ABC 4

item.title: Biden pardons the late Black nationalist Marcus Garvey as well as 4 others and commutes 2 sentences
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday posthumously pardoned Black nationalist Marcus Garvey, who influenced Malcolm X and other civil rights leaders and was convicted of mail fraud in the 1920s.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How Baltimore is saving lives by offering young men resources when they put down the guns
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — With his dad in prison and his mom suffering from alcoholism, Malik Grant faced abandonment and instability early on. He got used to people letting him down.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US cities largely saw a drop in violent crime in 2024, yet many still feel unsafe
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — As U.S. police departments release preliminary or finalized 2024 crime numbers, many are reporting historic declines in homicides and drops in other violent crimes compared to 2023. In many parts of the country, though, those decreases don't match the public perception.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Proposal would ditch BMI for determining obesity
item.description: According to a September 2024 data brief from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), 40.3 percent of U.S. adults were considered obese between August 2021 and August 2023.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Most voters in new survey seek toned-down Trump agenda
item.description: Over 60% of Americans polled disagree with Trump's idea to replace thousands of career civil-service workers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Family of Utah student who died hiking in Hawaii over winter break speaks out
item.description: "I know her goals in life were to protect lands and promote a healthy place to live for all of us. I can just speak in volumes to what an amazing, beautiful, strong woman she is.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Boneza, ABC 4

item.title: Extreme cold forecast for much of US in coming days
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — Another blast of winter weather is expected in parts of the U.S. in the coming days, including bone-chilling wind in the Northern Plains and unusual snow and ice in the Gulf Coast area. Cold weather forecasted for Monday for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago prepares for deportation arrests targeting hundreds next week after Trump takes office
item.description: Federal immigration officers will target more than 300 people with histories of egregious, violent crimes after President-elect Donald Trump takes office Monday, an official said, marking his administration's initial attempt toward fulfilling his promise of large-scale deportations.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wealthy Florida real estate brothers' 'trophies' uncovered as judge denies bail: feds
item.description: Three wealthy brothers are accused of drugging and sexually assaulting dozens of women in multiple states, and prosecutors said new video "trophies" have been seized as evidence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eggs are about to get even more expensive, expert says. Here's how much
item.description: Eggs are nearly 40% more expensive now than they were a year ago, the Labor Department said this week. It's about to get even worse, one expert says.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ will give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: President-elect Trump told NBC News on Saturday that he is likely to give TikTok a 90-day extension while his incoming administration assesses the situation following the Supreme Court decision to uphold a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Trump inauguration weekend commences as protesters rally in Washington
item.description: Inauguration festivities will begin in full force Saturday as President-elect Trump and his allies — and protesters — arrive in Washington ahead of Monday's inauguration of the 47th president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, Fox 5

item.title: Need to get a tattoo removed? What to know about the risks, the cost and more
item.description: When you’re young and carefree, a spur-of-the-moment tattoo feels like the ultimate form of self-expression. Years later, though, you might have a change of heart.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: A$AP Rocky’s shooting trial is set to begin. Here’s what to know about the case
item.description: A$AP Rocky is at a crossroads. He could soar to new heights of celebrity in 2025, or be brought back to earth quickly by a trial starting Tuesday that could lead to his imprisonment for years.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia appeals court sides with Trump in affirming dismissal of 6 state charges
item.description: A Georgia appeals court on Friday affirmed the dismissal of six counts in the state's 2020 election interference against President-elect Trump and his allies, upholding a trial judge's decision that the charges must be thrown out due to a lack of detail.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles water chief given police security detail amid 'threats' against her, employees
item.description: The Los Angeles water chief has been given a police security detail amid "threats" against her and water employees over the response to wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College students accused of online 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: Six Assumption University students were in court to face charges in a connection made between a Holyoke woman and a man on Tinder, which resulted in a TikTok plot to confront a falsely accused sexual predator.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Shook, WGN 9

item.title: 6 people injured after dump truck crashes into New Jersey Target
item.description: A dump truck crashed into a Target in New Jersey Friday, opening up a large hole in the side of the building and leaving several people injured.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court gives green light to law that could ban TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON — The Supreme Court on Friday delivered a blow to TikTok by upholding a law that could potentially lead to the video-sharing social media platform being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley and Kat Tenbarge, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Longer prison terms for human smugglers sought by US Justice Department
item.description: "We're trying to get the leaders of these smuggling groups to get longer sentences or those who work with cartels in Mexico to conduct their smuggling.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, WGN 9
